nodes.label: Base exception used by this module.
Base warning used by this module.
Base exception for errors caused within a pool.
Base exception for PoolErrors that have associated URLs.
Raised when SSL certificate fails in an HTTPS connection.
Raised when the connection to a proxy fails.
Raised when automatic decoding based on Content-Type fails.
Raised when something unexpected happens mid-request/response.
Raised when the maximum number of retries is exceeded.      :param pool: The con
Raised when an existing pool gets a request for a foreign host.
Raised when passing an invalid state to a timeout
Raised when a socket timeout error occurs.      Catching this error will catch b
Raised when a socket timeout occurs while receiving data from a server
Raised when a socket timeout occurs while connecting to a server
Raised when we fail to establish a new connection. Usually ECONNREFUSED.
Raised when host name resolution fails.
Raised when a pool runs out of connections and no more are allowed.
Raised when we try to add a connection to a full pool in blocking mode.
Raised when a request enters a pool after the pool has been closed.
Raised when there is something wrong with a given URL input.
Raised when get_host or similar fails to parse the URL input.
Raised when a URL input has an unsupported scheme.
Used as a container for an error reason supplied in a MaxRetryError.
Warned when performing security reducing actions
Warned when making an unverified HTTPS request.
Warned when using unsupported SSL library
Warned when system time is suspected to be wrong
Warned when certain TLS/SSL configuration is not available on a platform.
Warned when an attempt is made to import a module with missing optional     depe
Response needs to be chunked in order to read it as chunks.
Body should be :class:`http.client.HTTPResponse` like     (have an fp attribute
Response length doesn't match expected Content-Length      Subclass of :class:`h
Invalid chunk length in a chunked response.
The header provided was somehow invalid.
ProxyManager does not support the supplied scheme
Fetching HTTPS resources through HTTPS proxies is unsupported
Raised by assert_header_parsing, but we convert it to a log.warning statement.
urllib3 encountered an error when trying to rewind a body
